'''Jake Clifford''' (born 2 January 1998) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hb}} and {{rlp|fe}} for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] in the [[NRL]]. 

He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] and [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Clifford was born in [[Cairns]], Queensland, Australia and was raised in [[Tully, Queensland]] and is of [[Italian people|Italian]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/03/23/league-of-nations-stars-representing-40-countries/ |title=League of nations:Stars representing 40 countries |publisher=NRL.com |first=Brad |last=Walter |date=23 March 2022}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Tully Tigers]], before being signed by the [[North Queensland Cowboys]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/north-queensland-cowboys/jake-clifford/|title=Official NRL profile of Jake Clifford for North Queensland Cowboys|website=North Queensland Cowboys|language=en-AU|access-date=2018-05-09|archive-date=9 April 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190409054445/https://www.cowboys.com.au/teams/telstra-premiership/north-queensland-cowboys/jake-clifford/|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.cairnspost.com.au/sport/local-sport/tully-boy-jake-clifford-set-to-be-future-cowboys-star/news-story/2390aa5d4d764dd1b331765386f47e40?nk=d9247d1155df2d8dc103212cb9e2cb41-1525836404|title=Tully boy Jake Clifford set to be future Cowboys star|website=Cairns Post|date=13 February 2015 |access-date=2018-05-09}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2013, Clifford played for the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]]'s [[Cyril Connell Cup]] side. A year later, he captained the side in their Grand Final victory over Townsville.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2014/05/05/connell-cup-final---stingers-v-pride/|title=Connell Cup Final - Stingers v Pride|website=Queensland Rugby League|date=5 May 2014 }}</ref> In 2015, he moved up to the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]]'s [[Mal Meninga Cup]] side before moving to [[Townsville, Queensland|Townsville]] in 2016, where he played for the [[Townsville Blackhawks]]' Mal Meninga Cup side. In 2017, he played for the [[North Queensland Cowboys]] [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] side and was named in the 2017 NYC Team of the Year. On 27 September, he was named the 2017 Dally M NYC Player of the Year at the [[2017 Dally M Awards|Dally M awards]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/dally-m/dally-m-2017-cowboys-halfback-jake-clifford-wins-final-holden-cup-player-of-the-year-award/news-story/e9369a5b8df263d17910e241e0911c49|title=Clifford claims historic Holden Cup award|work=NewsComAu|access-date=2018-05-11}}</ref>

===2018===
On 17 February, he played off the interchange in a pre-season loss to the [[Wests Tigers]], scoring a try.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/02/17/Wests-Tigers-tame-North-Queensland-Cowboys-in-Cairns/|title=JT returns but Tigers tame Cowboys in Cairns|date=2018-02-17|work=National Rugby League|access-date=2018-05-09|language=en-AU}}</ref>

Clifford started the [[2018 NRL season]] playing for North Queensland's [[Queensland Cup]] feeder side, the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]]. On 16 April, he re-signed with the North Queensland club until the end of the [[2020 NRL season]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/cowboys-secure-johnathan-thurston-nrl-successor-with-ash-taylor-qualities/news-story/e8e3317b3a920ad23130db48b9afac41|title=Johnathan Thurston NRL successor: Cowboys half Jake Clifford has Ash Taylor qualities|date=2018-04-17|work=Fox Sports|access-date=2018-05-09|language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/04/17/north-queensland-cowboys-extend-young-star-jake-cliffords-contract-with-eye-on-post-johnathan-thurston-era/|title=North Queensland Cowboys extend young star Jake Clifford's contract with eye on post-Johnathan Thurston era|date=2018-04-16|work=National Rugby League|access-date=2018-05-09|language=en-AU}}</ref> On 24 June, he was 18th man for the [[Queensland Residents rugby league team|Queensland Residents]] side.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/20182/07/09/clifford-stands-tall-under-weight-of-great-expectations/|title=Clifford stands tall under weight of great expectations|date=2018-07-09|work=Queensland Rugby League|access-date=2018-07-12|language=en-AU}}</ref> On 11 July, he started at halfback for the [[Queensland under-20 rugby league team|Queensland U20]] side, scoring two tries and being named Man of the Match in Queensland's first ever win.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/20182/07/11/clifford-leads-the-way-in-qld-breakthrough/|title=Clifford leads the way in QLD breakthrough|date=2018-07-11|work=Queensland Rugby League|access-date=2018-07-12|language=en-AU}}</ref>

In Round 19 of the [[2018 NRL season]], he made his NRL debut against [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2018/07/21/final-team-list-cowboys-v-dragons/|title=Final team list: Cowboys v Dragons|date=21 July 2018|website=North Queensland Cowboys}}</ref>

On 29 August, he was named [[Intrust Super Cup]] Halfback of the Year and Rookie of the Year at the [[Queensland Rugby League|QRL]] Gala Awards night.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2018/08/29/clifford-named-q-cup-rookie-of-the-year/|title=Clifford named Q-Cup Rookie of the Year|date=29 August 2018|website=North Queensland Cowboys}}</ref> On 13 September, he was named the Cowboys Rookie of the Year at the club's presentation ball.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2018/09/13/our-2018-award-winners/|title=Our 2018 award winners!|date=13 September 2018|website=North Queensland Cowboys}}</ref>

On 6 October, Clifford earned his first senior representative honours, starting at five-eighth in the [[Prime Minister's XIII]]'s 34–16 win over [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/10/06/prime-ministers-xiii-score-hard-earned-win-over-papua-new-guinea-pms-xiii/|title=After pitch invasion, PM's XIII prevail|date=6 October 2018|website=National Rugby League}}</ref> A week later, on 13 October, Clifford captained the [[Junior Kangaroos]] to a 40–24 win over [[Junior Kiwis]], being named Man of the Match.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/10/13/junior-kangaroos-defeat-new-zealand-despite-incredible-try/|title=Junior Roos defeat Junior Kiwis despite incredible try|date=13 October 2018|website=National Rugby League}}</ref>

===2019===
Clifford entered the 2019 pre-season in a battle with [[Te Maire Martin]] for the vacant halves spot left by the retirement of [[Johnathan Thurston]]. Despite scoring a [[hat-trick]] in a pre-season trial against the [[Gold Coast Titans]], Clifford lost out to Martin and began the season playing for the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/cowboys/cowboy-clifford-trumps-half-rival-with-hattrick-against-titans/news-story/1e86dba4e0e3e3c25f9377f5eac21c4f|title=NRL 2019: North Queensland Cowboys Jake Clifford stars with hat-trick in trial win over Gold Coast Titans, pressure on Martin|date=23 February 2019}}</ref> He made his season debut in a Round 3 loss to the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], playing five games before being dropped back to the Pride.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-2019-round-8-cowboys-star-josh-mcguire-rages-after-injury-in-win-over-titans/news-story/a829da24441d6e28a7e3d07c0eaa6037|title=NRL 2019, Round 8 | Cowboys star Josh McGuire rages after injury in win over Titans|date=3 May 2019|website=NewsComAu}}</ref> Clifford returned to first grade in the Cowboys' Round 12 win over the [[Gold Coast Titans]]. In Round 13, he scored his first NRL points, kicking four goals in a 20–22 loss to the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].

In Round 17, he scored his first NRL try and kicked the game winning field goal in North Queensland's Round 17 win over the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.couriermail.com.au/subscribe/news/1/?sourceCode=CMWEB_WRE170_a&dest=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.couriermail.com.au%2Fsport%2Fnrl%2Fnorth-queensland-cowboys-upset-roosters-thanks-to-lastminute-jake-clifford-heroics%2Fnews-story%2F8c72fe2b08c674889134777280db332c&memtype=anonymous&mode=premium&nk=7a962d6e2f0676a8b9d7a3bd4693a117-1606193550|title=Couriermail.com.au | Subscribe to The Courier Mail for exclusive stories|website=www.couriermail.com.au}}</ref> In the Cowboys' Round 22 loss to the [[Newcastle Knights]], Clifford suffered an ankle injury and missed the final three games of the season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2019/08/22/updated-team-list-round-23-v-penrith/|title=Updated team list: Round 23 v Penrith|date=22 August 2019|website=North Queensland Cowboys}}</ref>

On 16 December, Clifford re-signed with North Queensland until the end of the [[2021 NRL season]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2019/12/16/clifford-signs-contract-extension/|title=Clifford signs contract extension|date=16 December 2019|website=North Queensland Cowboys}}</ref>

===2020===
In February, Clifford was a member of the Cowboys' [[2020 NRL Nines]] winning squad, scoring a try in their final win over the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/02/15/cowboys-out-gun-dragons-in-the-west/|title=Cowboys out-gun Dragons in the west to take Nines crown|date=15 February 2020|website=National Rugby League}}</ref> He began the 2020 season playing for the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]].

In Round 3, he returned to first grade, playing 10 games at halfback before being dropped by interim head coach [[Josh Hannay]] after a Round 12 loss to the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-round-3-team-news-draw-fixtures-injuries-team-lists-broncos-vs-eels-supercoach-updates/news-story/a925761078a9c4d9b349fac93260d791|title=NRL Teams Talking Points: Bennett's coy Bunnies switch, Titans shake up spine|date=27 May 2020|website=Fox Sports Australia}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/08/04/morgan-returns-norman-dropped-as-coaches-ring-changes/|title=Morgan returns, Norman dropped as coaches ring changes|date=4 August 2020|website=NRL}}</ref> In Round 17, he was recalled to the side, scoring a try in North Queensland's win over the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/08/31/cowboys-v-dragons-cliffords-half-chance-testing-time-for-brittain/|title=Cowboys v Dragons: Clifford at halfback; Fuimaono returns|date=6 September 2020|website=NRL}}</ref> Clifford finished the season as North Queensland's halfback, scoring a try in their Round 20 win over the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/09/24/brisbane-get-wooden-spoon-as-cowboys-finish-on-a-high/|title=Brisbane get wooden spoon as Cowboys finish on a high|date=24 September 2020|website=NRL}}</ref>

In November, Clifford signed a two-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] starting in 2022.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2020/11/24/jake-clifford-joins-knights-for-22-season/|title=Jake Clifford joins Knights for '22 season|date=24 November 2020|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

===2021===
On 29 May, after playing seven games with the North Queensland club, Clifford secured an early release to join [[Newcastle Knights|Newcastle]] for the remainder of the [[2021 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2021/05/29/jake-clifford-joins-knights-for-remainder-of-2021-season/|title=Jake Clifford joins Knights for remainder of 2021 season|date=29 May 2021|website=newcastleknights.com.au}}</ref>

He made his club debut in round 13 for Newcastle in a 40-4 loss against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-newcastle-knights-vs-parramatta-eels-live-blog-live-stream-supercoach-scores-videos-updates-reed-mahoney-jake-clifford/news-story/293e7170e956a8a17aa6a60124420d1d/|title=Milestone men Gutho and Sivo shine; Over-keen Knights 'panic' on old boys day: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=6 June 2021 }}</ref>

===2022===
Clifford made a total of 12 appearances for Newcastle in the [[2022 NRL season]] scoring two tries as the club finished 14th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2022-newcastle-knights-season-review/zuanch2lkdlbojvv3rtaafcg/|title=NRL 2022: Newcastle Knights season review|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=6 September 2022 }}</ref>

On 27 October, Clifford signed a two-year deal to join English side [[Hull F.C.]] starting in 2023.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-sign-nrl-halfback-7748142/|title=Hull FC sign NRL halfback from Newcastle Knights to complete new-look spine|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=27 October 2022|archive-date=27 October 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221027090431/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-sign-nrl-halfback-7748142|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2023===
Clifford made his club debut for Hull F.C. in round 1 of the 2023 Super League season against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] which ended in a 32-30 victory.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64668525|title=Super League: Hull FC 32-30 Castleford Tigers - Away side fall just short of remarkable fightback|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
On 2 August, Clifford signed a one-year deal to return to one of his former clubs, North Queensland for the 2024 NRL season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-nrl-transfer-centre-jack-cogger-knights-jackson-hastings-jayden-sullivan-rugby-league-news-reaction/news-story/c97bcb3132b91c49054081621e778b01/|title=Cowboys lure playmaker home; Knights poach Souths forward, Panthers half: Transfer Centre|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=3 August 2023 }}</ref>
Clifford played 25 games for Hull F.C. for in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=4 October 2023|archive-date=4 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2024===
Clifford was limited to only eight appearances with North Queensland in the [[2024 NRL season]] which saw the club finish 5th on the table. He played in both the club’s finals matches as they were eliminated in the second week by Cronulla.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-north-queensland-cowboys-season-review-20240924-p5kcxq.html/|title=The Mole: Cowboys show improvement in 2024 but worrying finals stat sparks thorny question|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>

===2025===
In round 4 of the [[2025 NRL season]], Clifford scored two tries for North Queensland in their 30-20 victory over Canberra.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2025-north-queensland-cowboys-v-canberra-raiders-score-stats-live-blog-supercoach-scores-news-is-todd-payten-on-hot-seat-scott-drinkwater-joseph-tapine/news-story/4d2d25090e6bf40cb5acf9356acbb882/|title=Canberra’s Achilles’ heel revealed; Bad habit Cowboys must iron out — Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref> On 28 July, North Queensland announced that Clifford had re-signed with the club on a one year extension.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-07-28 |title=Cowboys extend Clifford |url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2025/07/28/cowboys-extend-clifford-2026/ |access-date=2025-07-28 |website=North Queensland Cowboys |language=en}}</ref>
Clifford played 14 games for North Queensland in the [[2025 NRL season]] as the club finished 12th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2025-the-mole-season-review-north-queensland-cowboys-jason-taumalolo-deal-20250911-p5mugv.html/|title=The Mole's season review: 'Leaderless' Cowboys prove $10m gamble on Jason Taumalolo was a flop|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2026 ===
On 7 May, the Cowboys announced that Clifford had re-signed with the club for a further two years.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2026-05-06 |title=Clifford re-signs until the end of 2028 |url=https://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2026/05/07/cowboys-extend-clifford-jake-2028/ |access-date=2026-05-07 |website=North Queensland Cowboys |language=en}}</ref>
